seo.title: Montana Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Montana — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license montana, dental assistant certification montana, RDA montana, DANB CDA montana, dental assistant radiography montana

board.name: Montana Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address: 301 S Park Ave, 4th Floor, P.O. Box 200513, Helena, MT 59620-0513

quick_facts.renewal_fee: N/A - Montana does not license general Dental Assistants

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Montana.

steps.title: Understand Supervision Requirements
steps.description: Dental assistants in Montana perform duties under the direct or general supervision of a licensed dentist, depending on the specific task as defined by the Montana Board of Dentistry's administrative rules.

steps.title: Review Delegated Duties
steps.description: Familiarize yourself with the administrative rules of the Montana Board of Dentistry that outline the specific tasks a dental assistant can perform and the required level of supervision. These rules define the scope of practice for dental auxiliaries.

steps.title: Obtain Necessary Training and Competency
steps.description: While no general license exists, a dentist may only delegate tasks to a dental assistant whom they deem competent. Certain advanced functions (e.g., coronal polishing, sealant application) may require specific training or certification, as determined by the supervising dentist.

steps.title: Consider National Certifications (Optional)
steps.description: For career advancement or to demonstrate proficiency, consider obtaining national certifications such as the Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) credential from the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB), although this is not a state requirement for general practice in Montana.

steps.title: Comply with Dental Practice Act
steps.description: Ensure all duties performed comply with the Montana Dental Practice Act and associated administrative rules, which govern the practice of dentistry and the roles of auxiliary personnel in the state.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Montana does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Montana require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Montana does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Montana?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Montana.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Montana?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Montana?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Montana. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Montana
hero.dek: Montana does not license or register general Dental Assistants. There is no state application portal for basic dental assisting practice; dental assistants perform duties under the supervision of a licensed dentist.

official_links.portal_name: N/A - Montana does not license general Dental Assistants
official_links.registration_steps: Montana does not license or register general dental assistants. Therefore, there is no state application portal or registration process for basic dental assisting practice. Dental assistants in Montana perform duties under the supervision of a licensed dentist, adhering to the state's Dental Practice Act and administrative rules.
